canonical_question: How do you handle poison messages - triage and replay of failed ERP integration messages?
aliases: Poison message handling and dead letter queue triage for ERP integrations
How to triage and replay failed ERP integration messages from DLQ
Poison pill detection and remediation in enterprise middleware
DLQ triage workflow and message replay patterns for ERP

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: 2024-06 — AWS SQS DLQ Redrive API GA

constraints: Detect poison messages within 3-5 delivery attempts — fewer than 3 causes premature DLQ routing, more than 5 wastes processing capacity
DLQ retention varies by platform: AWS SQS max 14 days, Azure Service Bus unlimited (Premium), Kafka depends on topic config
Replay requires idempotency keys — without them, replay creates duplicate records in target ERP
Replay ordering matters — parent records must be replayed before child records to avoid referential integrity violations
DLQ-of-DLQ is an anti-pattern — never assign a DLQ to a DLQ consumer
MuleSoft Anypoint MQ DLQ must be in same region as source queue
Azure Service Bus DLQ messages persist indefinitely — no TTL, no automatic cleanup

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs general retry strategies and circuit breaker patterns
User needs idempotency key implementation for replay safety
User needs distributed transaction coordination across ERPs

depends_on.label: Error handling and DLQ fundamentals — retry strategies, exponential backoff, circuit breakers

related_to.label: Idempotency patterns — required for safe message replay
Change data capture — upstream event source that generates messages

solves.label: Order-to-cash integration — poison messages block order processing
Procure-to-pay integration — invoice failures require triage and replay

alternative_to.label: Saga pattern — for coordinated multi-step compensation, not message-level triage

often_confused_with.label: Error handling & DLQ (covers retry/backoff/circuit breaker; this card covers what happens AFTER messages reach the DLQ)

inputs_needed.question: Which message broker or middleware are you using?
What type of failures are you seeing?
options: Data quality errors (schema violations, missing required fields)
Transient errors (timeouts, rate limits, network issues)
Permanent errors (invalid API endpoint, authentication failures)

inputs_needed.question: Do you need to replay failed messages after fixing the root cause?
options: Yes — zero-loss, every message must eventually succeed
Yes — but only after manual review and approval
No — log and discard is acceptable
